description: Implement populateArcs: validate inputs, copy real arcs, and install artificial arcs (with supply/demand flows) into a freshly-allocated state.
commit: feat: populate state with real and artificial arcs
steps: Extend solver_state.go with `func populateArcs(s *state, arcs []Arc, src, sink int) error`. (Imports `fmt` if not already.)

Validate: s.n > 0; 0 <= src < s.n; 0 <= sink < s.n; src != sink; for each input arc, 0 <= Source < s.n, 0 <= Target < s.n, Capacity != nil. Return a wrapped error describing the first violation found.

Copy real arcs into indices [0, s.arcCount): for i, a := range arcs set arcSource[i]=a.Source, arcTarget[i]=a.Target, arcCost[i]=a.Cost, arcCap[i].Set(a.Capacity), arcFlow[i] is already zero from newEmptyState, arcState[i]=stateLower.

Compute supply upper bound S as the in-place sum of capacities of arcs leaving src: iterate real arcs; for each with Source==src, `S.Add(S, arcCap[i])`. Use a local *uint256.Int initialized via `new(uint256.Int)`.

Install the n artificial arcs at indices [s.arcCount, s.totalArcs). For each real node i in [0, s.n): let idx := s.arcCount + i. If i == src: arcSource[idx]=i, arcTarget[idx]=s.root, arcDir[idx]=dirDown (child i to parent root), arcFlow[idx].Set(S), arcCost[idx]= (math.MaxInt64/4). If i == sink: arcSource[idx]=s.root, arcTarget[idx]=i, arcDir[idx]=dirUp (root parent to child i), arcFlow[idx].Set(S), arcCost[idx]= (math.MaxInt64/4). Else: arcSource[idx]=i, arcTarget[idx]=s.root, arcDir[idx]=dirDown, arcFlow[idx] stays 0, arcCost[idx]= (math.MaxInt64/4). All artificials get arcState[idx]=stateTree and `arcCap[idx].Set(new(uint256.Int).Lsh(uint256.NewInt(1), 255))` as a near-unbounded capacity.

In solver_state_test.go add `TestPopulateArcs_Valid`: newEmptyState(3, 2), call populateArcs on a two-arc path graph (0->1 cap 10 cost 1; 1->2 cap 10 cost 1), src=0, sink=2. Assert all real arcs have the stated Source/Target/Cost/Cap and stateLower; artificial at index 2 has source=0 target=root flow=S state=stateTree; artificial at index 3 has flow=0 state=stateTree; artificial at index 4 has source=root target=2 flow=S state=stateTree.
Add `TestPopulateArcs_Errors` subtests: src==sink, out-of-range arc target, nil capacity. Each returns non-nil error.
acceptance_criteria: solver_state.go defines `populateArcs(s *state, arcs []Arc, src, sink int) error`.
After populateArcs, real arcs are in stateLower with unchanged flow, artificials are in stateTree with flows set per supply/demand.
